title: Back to the Smile
scripture_text: In your presence there is fullness of joy.
reflection: The gospel begins not with a king, but with a baby, in a feed trough, in a barn. God chose the bottom of the pile to enter the world. (The third hearing of this day.)
body:
The candles are lit. The songs are being sung. The children are asleep on the floor, dreaming of the morning.

Tonight, the world tilted. A long time ago, in a place you have never been, a woman gave birth, and the world broke open.

He was not what anyone expected. He came quietly, to the wrong family, in the wrong town, at the wrong time of year. He came to the bottom. He came to the people no one was looking at.

And that is good news, tonight, for you. The God who runs the universe has been, for a long time, on the side of the overlooked. Of the small. Of the tired. Of you.

Sleep well. He is near.

The joy of the Lord is not a happy feeling. The joy of the Lord is something deeper, something that does not require the circumstances to be right. It is the bedrock. It is what is there, under the surface, even when the surface is sad.

You have felt this joy. You have felt it in a room that was otherwise heavy. You have felt it in a song that did not make sense at first. You have felt it on a day you did not want to wake up. It came anyway. The joy came anyway. That is the joy. That is the Lord.

It will come again. Even today. Especially today. It comes like the sun over the hill, on its own time, in its own way. You cannot earn it. You cannot make it. You can only be there when it arrives.

The gospel begins in a feed trough, in a barn, in the wrong town, at the wrong time of year. The Lord did not start in a palace. He started in the bottom of the pile, with the people no one was looking at, and He has not left. He is with the bottom of the pile still. He is with you.

The joy came. It will come again. Be there when it does.

This is the third time the year has come around to this day. You have been here before. The Lord has been here before. The body remembers. The soul remembers. The grace is the same.
prayer: Lord, You came. Thank You for coming. Thank You for coming as a baby, so we would not be afraid. Amen.
